root_sections.title:
Figure 37: Changes in citizens’ understanding of the FTA initiative
44
root_sections.summary: The share of Ethiopian citizens who know their local government budget and expenditure rose from 9 % in 2009 to 62 % in 2022 (a seven‑fold increase), although it modestly declined from the 2016 peak of 66 % in tandem with the 2020 political reforms, heightened instability, and the COVID‑19 pandemic. Knowledge is highly heterogeneous across regions (80 % in Addis Ababa versus 47 % in Oromia) and strongly correlated with formal education (68 % among respondents with at least primary schooling), with Amhara, Oromia, Gambella, Benishangul‑Gumuz, and Somali regions showing notable drops relative to 2016.
